title: quercus robur subsp. robur
intro: Quercus robur subsp. robur, also known as English oak, is a subspecies of the deciduous oak tree that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus robur subsp. robur have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving cognitive function, and supporting immune system health. Quercus robur subsp. robur is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for use in herbal medicine, and is also used in the production of oak bark extracts and other traditional herbal preparations.
